$comment: Registry of upstream extension-sample repositories. The weekly QA job clones each enabled source on-demand into .cache/<id> (gitignored), pins the tested commit in sources.lock.json, discovers samples under `scan`, and runs `extension build` against each. `layout` controls discovery: 'manifest-root' = a sample is any dir directly containing manifest.json (MDN/Chrome style); 'project-root' = each immediate child of a scan dir is a sample whose manifest lives somewhere beneath (e.g. src/manifest.json, Extension.js style). `vendorDir` names the tracked corpus dir vendor.mjs promotes validated snapshots into (the repo's browsable data product; see catalog.json). `vendorInclude` lists extra trees vendored for data value but excluded from the matrix; `vendorFrozen` marks a tracked corpus with no upstream sync (stamped + integrity-checked, never copied); `vendorScan` overrides scan for cataloging a frozen corpus. Add a new browser ecosystem by adding an entry here: no code changes needed.

sources.name: Chromium test extensions
sources.note: Chromium has no standalone samples repo; cloning the full tree is huge. The tracked chromium/ dir is a frozen curated subset of the upstream test extensions (vendored 2023-06, stamped + integrity-checked, catalog-only). Enable live sync only with a sparse-checkout strategy (planned).

sources.name: Microsoft Edge extension samples
sources.note: Candidate upstream: verify the repo URL and that sample dirs carry manifest.json before enabling. Edge consumes Chromium MV3, so `--browser edge` is the relevant target.

sources.name: Opera extension samples
sources.note: Opera's sample repos are largely unmaintained; URL must be confirmed before enabling. Opera is Chromium-based, so `--browser chromium` covers it.
